sentence: Writing more than two centuries ago, Adam Smith introduced his concept of 'the invisible hand,' which went on to become one of the most celebrated and influential ideas of all time.
translation: 2世紀以上前に執筆し, アダム・スミスは「見えざる手」という概念を導入したが, それは史上最も有名で影響力のある考えの一つとなった。

sentence: His idea was that individuals seeking to promote only their own interests in the marketplace would be driven, "as if by an invisible hand," to promote the greatest good for all.
translation: 彼の考えは, 市場において自分の利益だけを追求しようとする個人が, 「あたかも見えざる手によって」導かれ, 結果としてすべての人にとって最大の利益をもたらすことになるということだった。

sentence: Thus producers seeking to steal a market share from their rivals would develop cost-reducing innovations, which would be copied by others, and which in time would lead to lower prices for all.
translation: したがって, 競合から市場シェアを奪おうとする生産者はコスト削減の革新的な技術を開発し, それが他者に模倣され, やがてすべての人にとっての低価格につながる。

sentence: Farmers rush to adopt higher yielding varieties of corn of mixed species; cattlemen rush to adopt faster growing breeds of cattle; and long-distance truckers rush to install fuel-saving air foils on the top of their cabs.
translation: 農家は収穫量の多い混合種のトウモロコシ品種を急いで導入し, 畜産農家は成長の早い牛の品種を急いで導入し, 長距離トラック運転手はキャブの頂上に燃料節約用のエアフォイルを急いで設置する。
